---
species: "Littorina obtusata"
accepted_code: "LIOB"
alternate_codes: 
diminutive: 
alternate_diminutives: 
common_name: "Yellow Periwinkle or Flat Periwinkle"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Small marine gastropod with a brightly colored yellow to yellow-green shell"
image: '/header_images/Littorina_obtusata_GuernseyAlderney_worms.jpg'
image_credit: 'Photo from Alderney Guernsey via [WORMS](https://malacopics.nl/showimage.php?malacopicname=Littorinidae&image=Littorina%20obtusata%20(f)%20aurantia%20(4).jpg)'
categories:
  - gastropods
sampling_programs:
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
habitat:
  - intertidal
mobility:
  - mobile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 24000000
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 140263 
---


**Other images:** 

![Alt image of hiru](/images/littorina_obtusata_JuliusA.Ellrich_Worms.jpg)
<br/>
Image by Julius A. Ellrich from [Worms](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=113220&tid=140263)
<br/>


**Distinguishing features:** Small intertidal snail with a brightly colored yellow to yellow-green shell. Has a flattened spire and a tear-drop-shaped opening. 

<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Littorina saxatilis* (LISA) and *Littorina littorea* (LILI). LIOB is the most distinctly yellow of the three. LILI is pointy and cone-shaped, LISA is oval with a bumpy texture, and LIOB is round with a flat spine. LILI can grow larger, and LISA is the smallest of the three. 

<br/>

**Extended Description:** 

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=140263)**
